per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA describes use of Google Analytics and transmission of website traffic data, while the REG requires the controller to facilitate exercise of data subject rights; these do not clearly express the same or directly conflicting constraint.

The REA states that the website uses Google Analytics and transmits traffic data to Google servers in the United States, but it does not clearly address whether processing is lawful only if consent or another legal basis applies; omission alone is not a deviation.
The REA concerns analytics usage and traffic data transmission, whereas the REG grants a right to erasure without undue delay; there is no quote-supported overlap or direct conflict.
